title: Grafana Dashboard Render Time, Panel Query Duration, and User-Perceived Load-Time P95 Trending

premiumApps.name: Splunk ITSI
premiumApps.note: optional — group episodes by grafana_instance and dashboard_uid entities when KPIs consume the same metrics indexes

dataSources: `index=grafana` or `index=observability` with `sourcetype=grafana:access` for `/api/dashboards/uid/*`, `/d/*`, and `/render` paths including `duration_ms`, `org_id`, `user`, `dashboard_uid`, and `status_code`; `sourcetype=grafana:logs` for structured lines with `logger=tsdb.prometheus`, `logger=plugin.querydata`, or query-data namespaces carrying `duration_ms`, `panel_id`, `datasource`, and `dashboard_uid` when present; `index=metrics` or `prometheus_metrics` with `mstats` on `metric_name` matching `grafana_api_dashboard_get_milliseconds_*` and `grafana_api_dashboard_save_milliseconds_*` scraped from Grafana `/metrics`; optional `index=splunk_rum` or `index=webperf` with `sourcetype=splunk_rum:span` or synthetic `sourcetype=browser:synthetic` for Grafana iframe wrapper or Selenium journeys tagging `service=grafana_ui`; lookups `grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv`; `index=cmdb` `sourcetype=grafana_dashboard_metadata` for deduped dashboard_uid enrichment.
app: Grafana OSS Enterprise Cloud plus Splunk OpenTelemetry Collector or Universal Forwarder log pipelines, Prometheus scrape into Splunk metrics indexes, optional Splunk RUM or synthetic browser ingest
description: Detects Grafana dashboards whose end-to-end load-time P95 breaches declared service levels by correlating reverse-proxy or Grafana access timing for dashboard JSON and render endpoints, structured backend logs for slow tsdb and plugin query-data paths, internal Grafana histogram metrics from the Prometheus `/metrics` surface, and optional real-user or synthetic browser vitals for embedded Grafana experiences, then scoring slowest panels by datasource and query shape, anonymous versus authenticated render deltas, error-panel share, and frontend regressions after plugin or version upgrades.
value: Platform and observability leaders see which executive and incident dashboards become operationally unusable before chatter in chat rooms turns into pager storms, because percentile load time, panel query tails, and auth-path latency separate a noisy neighbor tenant from a toxic dashboard import. Product and site reliability stakeholders receive owner-scoped evidence that names the dashboard UID, the business priority class, and the recommended remediation branch instead of a generic slow website complaint.
grandmaExplanation: Picture a mission control room where every dashboard is a wall of televisions. When channel changes drag on, operators miss the action. We time how long the whole wall wakes up, which screen lags worst, and whether guests see a faster picture than signed-in staff, so crews fix the right feed before frustration turns into blind flying.
implementation: Land grafana:access and grafana:logs with duration and UID fields, scrape Grafana /metrics into mstats-ready indexes, optionally mirror RUM or synthetic spans tagged for Grafana UI, publish grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv and cmdb grafana_dashboard_metadata, save observability_uc_13_6_1_grafana_dashboard_load_panel_perf on five-minute cadence with earliest=-4h@h, route critical and high severities to platform observability on-call, and archive weekly CSV exports to a restricted evidence index.
detailedImplementation:
Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with observability platform engineers who operate Grafana OSS, Grafana Enterprise, and Grafana Cloud estates, the teams that run Prometheus, Loki, Tempo, and Mimir backends queried by those dashboards, Kubernetes platform squads scheduling Grafana pods, and reverse-proxy owners terminating TLS on nginx or Envoy in front of Grafana. This use case is deliberately not UC-13.6.2, which isolates data source query failures and blank panels. It is not UC-13.6.3, which isolates Unified Alerting scheduler duration and missed evaluation ticks. It is not UC-13.6.5, which isolates login, session, and identity abuse narratives. It is not UC-13.6.13, which isolates notifier delivery failures after alerts already fired. Here the question is whether humans waiting on Grafana can act: HTML and JSON arrival times, panel query tails, render-server bottlenecks for PNG exports, anonymous snapshot viewers versus authenticated operators, and post-upgrade frontend regressions that show up as larger Core Web Vitals in embedded iframes even when raw PromQL looks healthy.

Grafana access logs deserve first-class teaching for every responder. Modern deployments often terminate TLS at nginx, HAProxy, Envoy, or a cloud load balancer that writes structured JSON per request. Paths of interest include `/api/dashboards/uid/<uid>` for metadata retrieval, `/d/<uid>/<slug>` for browser navigation, and `/render` family endpoints used by image renderers and alerting. Latency may appear as `duration_ms`, `request_time`, `upstream_response_time`, or split queue versus upstream fields; normalize before detection runs. User identifiers may be absent for anonymous orgs, literal `anonymous` strings, or service accounts used by renderers; treat those cohorts separately so kiosk boards do not pollute authenticated SLOs. Status codes matter because a fast 401 still frustrates operators; pair latency with `status_code` classes for error_panel_pct style rollups on the access lane.

Structured Grafana logs complement access lines because they carry query internals. `logger=tsdb.prometheus` and `logger=tsdb.loki` style namespaces record datasource identifiers, query fragments, and durations when log level permits. `plugin.querydata` and related plugin loggers surface panel-level `panel_id`, `datasource_uid`, and `duration_ms` that rarely appear on nginx lines. Enterprise query caching and read-only replicas change where time accumulates: sometimes the log shows a fast tsdb hop but the browser still waits on transformation plugins or repeated annotation queries. Repeat-by-variable panel multiplication can explode cardinality of panel instances; your parsers should retain enough identifiers to distinguish exploded series without storing full query text in evidence packs.

Grafana exposes Prometheus metrics on `/metrics` including histograms such as `grafana_api_dashboard_get_milliseconds_bucket` and save paths such as `grafana_api_dashboard_save_milliseconds_bucket`, plus database counters like `grafana_database_query_milliseconds_count` that help separate API wall time from SQL pressure. Scrape relabel rules must preserve `instance` or `pod` labels that map to `grafana_instance` in this search. Federation and remote write layers should not collapse labels prematurely or multisearch arms lose accountability.

Dashboard provisioning and GitOps reloads can spike load without malicious intent. When provisioning watches hot-reload large folders, Grafana may re-query datasources and recompute permissions; access P95 rises across many UIDs simultaneously. Treat those windows as expected only when change metadata confirms an approved apply; otherwise this detection still pages because user-visible pain is real.

Optional real-user instrumentation differs from UC-13.5.9, which targets your first-party web applications instrumented with Splunk RUM by default. For Grafana-as-product monitoring, instrument a thin host shell or iframe wrapper page that loads Grafana, or run synthetic Selenium journeys that record Largest Contentful Paint and Interaction to Next Paint into `splunk_rum:span` or a `browser:synthetic` sourcetype with `service=grafana_ui`. Those spans reveal client bundle regressions after plugin upgrades and CDN cache misses that server logs under-report.

RBAC and privacy reviews must settle whether dashboard titles, usernames, and datasource UIDs may appear in alert narratives. Inventory CSV files should avoid secrets; store UIDs, owner teams, business priority, sla_class, runbook_url, and last_reviewed only. Break-glass paths belong in vaults with rotation telemetry correlated separately.

Risk briefing: marketing demos and executive read-only accounts can create bursty traffic that is legitimate yet noisy; use business_priority in lookups to tune routing. Shared Grafana Cloud cells may show correlated spikes during vendor maintenance; compare vendor status before internal blame. Image renderer pools exhaust independently from query paths; keep renderer queue metrics adjacent in runbooks even though this UC emphasizes user-perceived load.

Differentiation recap: UC-13.6.1 focuses on end-user dashboard performance: total load time P95, slowest panel query tails by datasource, anonymous versus authenticated rendering deltas, error share proxies, and optional browser vitals for Grafana surfaces. It does not replace datasource error-rate analytics, alerting scheduler analytics, authentication anomaly analytics, or notifier delivery analytics in sibling gold cards.

Collector posture: stamp `change_ticket_id` onto forwarded events when Grafana versions, plugins, or renderer images change. When log volume is high, route `grafana:access` to an observability index with hot retention tuned for investigations. Clock skew between proxies and Grafana pods widens bogus duration comparisons; monitor NTP discipline elsewhere.

Field normalization expectations: `dashboard_uid` must join `grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv` and `grafana_dashboard_metadata`. If your proxies only log slugs, derive UIDs from a catalog feed before this search executes. `panel_id` may be numeric or string; coerce consistently. `datasource` should prefer `datasource_type` or `datasource_uid` fragments that map to prometheus, loki, tempo, mixed, or cloudwatch families for recommended_action tokens.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five complementary lanes the multisearch stanza fans in parallel, plus two knowledge objects for enrichment.

Lane A lands `sourcetype=grafana:access` into `index=grafana` or a shared observability index. Ensure URI paths retain case-insensitive matching for `/api/dashboards/uid/`, `/d/`, and `/render`. Extract `dashboard_uid` from URI when not already a field. Map `duration_ms` from JSON or key-value pairs; if only seconds exist, multiply safely. Preserve `org_id`, `user`, `status_code`, and upstream cluster hints.

Lane B tails `sourcetype=grafana:logs` with JSON or logfmt parsing that preserves `logger`, `duration_ms`, `panel_id`, `datasource`, `datasource_uid`, and `dashboard_uid` when present. TRUNCATE must survive long query excerpts in labs. FIELDALIAS should copy mixed-case Grafana fields into stable names.

Lane C scrapes Grafana `/metrics` into `prometheus_metrics` or `metrics` indexes with `mstats`-compatible `metric_name` values for `grafana_api_dashboard_get_milliseconds_bucket`, `grafana_api_dashboard_save_milliseconds_bucket`, and related counters. Preserve labels that identify instance or pod identity for `grafana_instance`.

Lane D is optional but recommended: ingest Splunk RUM spans or synthetic browser results with `service=grafana_ui` or `app=grafana_iframe` into `splunk_rum` or `webperf` indexes. Include `lcp_ms`, `inp_ms`, and navigation duration fields when available.

Lane E can remain folded into Lane D; if you lack RUM, duplicate Lane A with a stricter filter on `/public/dashboards` anonymous paths to amplify anon cohorts. For simplicity, this deployment guide treats Lane D as the optional fifth multisearch arm in Step 3.

Knowledge object one maintains `lookups/grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv` with columns `dashboard_uid`, `dashboard_title`, `owner_team`, `business_priority`, `sla_class`, `runbook_url`, and `last_reviewed`. Refresh weekly from Grafana HTTP API exports or GitOps registries under change control.

Knowledge object two maintains `index=cmdb` `sourcetype=grafana_dashboard_metadata` with deduped `dashboard_uid` rows carrying `environment`, `provisioning_source`, and optional `notes` fields for enrichment joins.

Example curl helpers from a bastion during design reviews:

```bash
curl -fsS https://grafana.example.org/metrics | rg -n "grafana_api_dashboard_get_milliseconds"
curl -fsS -H "Authorization: Bearer $GRAFANA_TOKEN" https://grafana.example.org/api/dashboards/uid/dash_abc | jq '.dashboard.uid,.meta.slug'
```

Pre-save validation: confirm each sourcetype produces events in a one-hour window, that intentional slow dashboard loads in lab increase access `duration_ms` within one collection interval, and that `grafana:logs` prints `plugin.querydata` durations when query inspector shows slow panels.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as `observability_uc_13_6_1_grafana_dashboard_load_panel_perf` with a five-minute schedule during steady production, `dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, and alert when `severity` is `critical` or `high`. Throttle duplicate `grafana_instance` and `dashboard_uid` pairs for thirty minutes unless severity escalates. Include `recommended_action`, `slowest_panel_ratio`, and `error_panel_pct` fragments in pager narratives so incident commanders open dashboard and datasource runbooks instead of generic home pages.

Pipeline narrative: `multisearch` fans Grafana access timing, structured tsdb and plugin query logs, internal Grafana API histogram metrics via `mstats`, optional RUM or synthetic browser spans for Grafana UI, and a fifth arm that duplicates access with anonymous viewer bias fields when RUM is absent so anon versus auth deltas still exist. After union, `coalesce()` fills missing `dashboard_uid`, `panel_id`, `datasource`, and `owner_team` fallback values across field naming drift. Time bucketing and `stats` compute `load_time_p95_ms`, `panel_query_p99_ms`, `panel_count`, and error shares. `streamstats` implements a one-hour sliding window per `dashboard_uid` on five-minute buckets to approximate sustained SLO breaches and to stabilize `slowest_panel_ratio`. `eventstats` adds environment baselines for load percentiles to catch noisy-neighbor regressions on shared cells. `case()` encodes criticality when load P95 remains above five seconds for thirty minutes or when a single panel query tail consumes more than sixty percent of wall time while panel P99 exceeds eight seconds. `inputlookup` joins inventory titles and SLA class. A `join` to `grafana_dashboard_metadata` adds provisioning context. The closing `table` lists audit-ready columns for governance screenshots.

Fenced SPL must match the top-level `spl` JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.6.1 Grafana dashboard load P95 panel query P99 anon vs auth render slowest_panel_ratio grafana:access grafana:logs mstats RUM optional earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m stream_window=12")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform) sourcetype=grafana:access earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval uri=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,request_uri,url_path,path,""))))
      | where like(uri,"%/api/dashboards/uid/%") OR like(uri,"%/d/%") OR like(uri,"%/render%")
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,splunk_server,"unknown_grafana"))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval user_f=trim(toString(coalesce(user,remote_user,username,http_user,"")))
      | eval auth_kind=if(len(user_f)<2 OR user_f=="-" OR match(lower(user_f),"anonymous|anon|guest|public"),"anon","auth")
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,dashboardUid,uid,"")))
      | rex field=uri max_match=1 "/api/dashboards/uid/(?<rex_uid>[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)"
      | eval dashboard_uid=if(len(dashboard_uid)<2 AND len(rex_uid)>1,rex_uid,dashboard_uid)
      | rex field=uri max_match=1 "/d/(?<rex_dash>[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)/"
      | eval dashboard_uid=if(len(dashboard_uid)<2 AND len(rex_dash)>1,rex_dash,dashboard_uid)
      | eval load_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,request_time_ms,latency_ms,upstream_response_time_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval load_ms=if(load_ms>0 AND load_ms<3000000,load_ms,null())
      | eval panel_id=trim(toString(coalesce(panel_id,"access_aggregate")))
      | eval datasource=trim(toString(coalesce(datasource,"nginx_access_path")))
      | eval query_ms=null()
      | eval err_hit=if(match(toString(coalesce(status_code,http_status,status,"200")),"^(4|5)"),1,0)
      | eval lane="access_auth"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform) sourcetype=grafana:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(toString(coalesce(_raw,"")))
      | eval lg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(logger,component,""))))
      | where like(lg,"%tsdb.prometheus%") OR like(lg,"%tsdb.loki%") OR like(lg,"%plugin.querydata%") OR match(lr,"querydata|tsdb\\.prometheus|tsdb\\.loki")
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,splunk_server,"unknown_grafana"))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,dashboardUid,uid,"panel_unknown")))
      | eval panel_id=trim(toString(coalesce(panelId,panel_id,"0")))
      | eval datasource=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(datasource,datasource_type,ds_type,"mixed"))))
      | eval query_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,query_duration_ms,elapsed_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval query_ms=if(query_ms>0 AND query_ms<600000,query_ms,null())
      | eval load_ms=null()
      | eval auth_kind="backend"
      | eval err_hit=if(match(lr,"error|eror|fail|timeout|deadline|canceled"),1,0)
      | eval lane="tsdb_plugin_logs"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ | mstats avg(_value) AS hist_ms WHERE index=metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics OR index=observability_metrics (metric_name="grafana_api_dashboard_get_milliseconds_bucket" OR metric_name="grafana_api_dashboard_save_milliseconds_bucket") span=5m BY host metric_name le
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,"metrics_host"))))
      | eval org_id="0"
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,"metrics_aggregate")))
      | eval panel_id="metrics_histogram"
      | eval datasource="grafana_internal_metrics"
      | eval le_num=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(le,le_bucket,"0"))),10)
      | eval query_ms=null()
      | eval load_ms=if(isnotnull(le_num),le_num,null())
      | eval auth_kind="metrics"
      | eval err_hit=0
      | eval lane="grafana_api_histogram"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=splunk_rum OR index=webperf OR index=observability) (sourcetype=splunk_rum:span OR sourcetype=browser:synthetic) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service.name,service,""))))
      | eval app_l=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app,application,""))))
      | where match(svc,"grafana") OR match(app_l,"grafana") OR match(lower(coalesce(url_full,page_url,"")),"grafana")
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,deployment_environment,"rum_grafana"))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,"0")))
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,route,http_target,"rum_dashboard")))
      | eval panel_id="rum_navigation"
      | eval datasource="browser_rum"
      | eval load_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,page_load_ms,largest_contentful_paint_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval query_ms=null()
      | eval auth_kind=if(match(lower(coalesce(user_status,auth_state,"")),"anon|guest|anonymous"),"anon","auth")
      | eval err_hit=if(match(lower(coalesce(error_type,exception_type,"")),"error|exception"),1,0)
      | eval lane="grafana_rum_optional"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability) sourcetype=grafana:access earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval uri=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,request_uri,url_path,path,""))))
      | where like(uri,"%/public/dashboards%") OR like(uri,"%/api/public%")
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,splunk_server,"unknown_grafana"))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval user_f="anonymous_viewer"
      | eval auth_kind="anon"
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,"public_board")))
      | eval load_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,request_time_ms,latency_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval panel_id="public_surface"
      | eval datasource="anonymous_edge"
      | eval query_ms=null()
      | eval err_hit=if(match(toString(coalesce(status_code,http_status,status,"200")),"^(4|5)"),1,0)
      | eval lane="access_anon_bias"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
| eval grafana_instance=coalesce(grafana_instance,"unknown_grafana")
| eval dashboard_uid=coalesce(nullif(dashboard_uid,""),"unknown_uid")
| eval panel_id=coalesce(panel_id,"0")
| eval datasource=coalesce(datasource,"mixed")
| eval org_id=coalesce(org_id,"0")
| eval auth_kind=coalesce(auth_kind,"auth")
| stats
    perc95(load_ms) AS load_time_p95_ms
    perc99(query_ms) AS panel_query_p99_ms
    max(query_ms) AS max_panel_query_ms
    count AS evt_count
    dc(panel_id) AS panel_count
    sum(err_hit) AS err_hits
    perc95(eval(if(auth_kind=="anon",load_ms,null()))) AS anon_load_p95_ms
    perc95(eval(if(auth_kind=="auth",load_ms,null()))) AS auth_load_p95_ms
  BY _time grafana_instance org_id dashboard_uid datasource lane
| eval error_panel_pct=if(evt_count>0,round(100*err_hits/evt_count,2),0)
| eval slowest_panel_ratio=round(max_panel_query_ms/nullif(load_time_p95_ms,0),4)
| eval slowest_panel_ratio=if(isnull(slowest_panel_ratio),0,slowest_panel_ratio)
| sort 0 grafana_instance dashboard_uid datasource - _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f
    first(load_time_p95_ms) AS load_p95_lead
    first(max_panel_query_ms) AS max_q_lead
    sum(eval(if(load_time_p95_ms>5000,1,0))) AS over5k_buckets
  BY grafana_instance dashboard_uid
| eval sustained_load_critical=if(over5k_buckets>=6,1,0)
| eventstats median(load_time_p95_ms) AS env_load_med BY grafana_instance
| eval severity=case(
    (sustained_load_critical==1 AND load_time_p95_ms>5000) OR (slowest_panel_ratio>0.6 AND panel_query_p99_ms>8000),"critical",
    load_time_p95_ms>3500 OR panel_query_p99_ms>5000 OR slowest_panel_ratio>0.45 OR error_panel_pct>8 OR (anon_load_p95_ms>0 AND auth_load_p95_ms>0 AND anon_load_p95_ms<(auth_load_p95_ms*0.55)),"high",
    load_time_p95_ms>2200 OR panel_query_p99_ms>2500 OR error_panel_pct>3 OR load_time_p95_ms>(env_load_med*1.6),"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND slowest_panel_ratio>0.6,"rewrite_or_shard_heaviest_panel_raise_recording_rules_narrow_time_range",
    severity=="critical" AND sustained_load_critical==1,"investigate_grafana_db_and_api_path_scale_replicas_review_provisioning_reload_storms",
    match(datasource,"loki"),"tighten_logql_line_filters_reduce_scan_width_review_chunk_and_query_limits",
    match(datasource,"prometheus|mimir"),"inspect_federation_and_remote_read_paths_dedupe_high_cardinality_queries",
    match(datasource,"browser_rum"),"profile_frontend_bundle_and_plugin_assets_validate_cdn_cache_headers",
    match(lane,"access_anon_bias"),"compare_anon_versus_auth_paths_tune_cookie_and_oauth_proxy_overhead",
    true(),"open_grafana_query_inspector_correlate_nginx_grafana_logs_and_metrics")
| join type=left max=0 dashboard_uid
    [| inputlookup grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv
     | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,uid,"")))
     | fields dashboard_uid dashboard_title owner_team business_priority sla_class runbook_url last_reviewed]
| join type=left max=0 dashboard_uid
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=grafana_dashboard_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
     | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,uid,"")))
     | dedup dashboard_uid
     | fields dashboard_uid environment provisioning_source]
| eval dashboard_title=coalesce(dashboard_title,dashboard_uid)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,business_priority,"standard")
| table _time grafana_instance dashboard_uid dashboard_title owner_team sla_class load_time_p95_ms panel_query_p99_ms slowest_panel_ratio error_panel_pct severity recommended_action
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[observability_uc_13_6_1_grafana_dashboard_load_panel_perf_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
action.email = 1
action.email.to = platform-observability-oncall@example.com
action.email.subject = Grafana dashboard perf $result.severity$ uid=$result.dashboard_uid$
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch observability_uc_13_6_1_grafana_dashboard_load_panel_perf | where severity IN ("critical","high")
```

Performance: if Job Inspector shows multisearch queue time above internal targets, materialize each lane into a five-minute summary index keyed on `grafana_instance`, `dashboard_uid`, and `datasource`, widen detection windows on summaries, and retain this full search for investigations. For Grafana Cloud estates with extreme cardinality, split searches by `org_id` using a macro token.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins in Grafana: open the dashboard, enable query inspector on the slowest panel, and compare recorded durations to `grafana:logs` rows within the same five-minute bucket. Compare nginx or Envoy access lines for `/api/dashboards/uid/...` to browser network waterfalls; if Splunk is quiet while the UI lags, verify sourcetype routing and timestamp zones.

Metrics validation: query Prometheus or Splunk metrics views for `grafana_api_dashboard_get_milliseconds_bucket` movement and confirm histogram shifts align with access P95 trends. When histograms disagree, fix scrape duplication or label collisions before muting.

RUM validation: when optional browser spans exist, compare Largest Contentful Paint movement to plugin upgrade timelines and CDN purge windows.

Synthetic positive test: in lab, import a community dashboard with an unbounded table panel, load it repeatedly, and confirm `high` or `critical` severity within two windows with non-null `recommended_action`.

Synthetic negative test: remove the toxic panel, restore caching, and confirm severities return to `low` for steady state with zero critical rows in the alert wrapper for thirty consecutive runs.

Runbook linkage: document expected spikes during Grafana upgrades when JS bundles repopulate edge caches; validators compare against approved maintenance calendars.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Repeat-by-variable panel exploded combinatorially after a deploy: roll back the variable defaults, split the panel into smaller units, and promote recording rules so each repeated query reads cheap series.

Case 2 — Loki query uses a seven-day range with a broad line filter causing thirty-second panel queries: narrow the interval, push filters closer to stream selectors, and raise limits only after capacity review with logging owners.

Case 3 — Prometheus federation across regions adds multi-second remote-read latency: collapse federation hops, align scrape intervals, and duplicate critical recording rules locally.

Case 4 — Grafana plugin upgrade ships a heavier client bundle: compare RUM LCP regressions, pin plugin versions temporarily, and file vendor issues with bundle diffs.

Case 5 — CDN purge during release cold-misses static assets: warm caches from a canary region before broad announcements, and extend SLO windows during approved purges.

Case 6 — Auth proxy adds one point five seconds overhead for authenticated dashboards but anonymous snapshots stay fast: tune oauth2-proxy or Envoy ext_authz budgets, enable session caching where safe, and document expected anon versus auth deltas.

Case 7 — SaaS Grafana noisy neighbor on a shared cell elevates everyone’s P95: engage vendor support with tenant identifiers, temporarily move executive boards to a dedicated stack, and compare neighbor spikes using instance-level metrics.

Case 8 — Rendered PNG worker queue starvation breaks alert images while UI loads: scale renderer replicas, isolate alert rendering pools, and verify image plugin health separately from query paths.

Case 9 — Community dashboard import brings an unreviewed heavy table without LIMIT: add limits, convert to recording rules, and block imports without peer review in GitOps.

Case 10 — Ten monitors on one NOC wall all open the same heavy dashboard during an incident: duplicate read-only summaries with fewer panels, enable kiosk snapshots, and educate operators to use playbooks instead of maximal boards.

Case 11 — Iframe embedding from another tenant crosses slow cross-origin auth: align Content Security Policy, shorten token exchange chains, and avoid double authentication layers.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the `spl` field exactly; multisearch lists five arms; required SPL verbs appear; closing `table` lists twelve operational columns including SLA class and recommended action.

Teaching notes for junior engineers: access P95 tells you the user waits; tsdb logs tell you which datasource type starved; internal metrics tell you whether Grafana service time or SQL grew; RUM tells you whether the browser world diverged after a plugin or CDN event. Combine all four before blaming a single team.

Query caching in Enterprise can mask cold-cache spikes after maintenance; pair this UC with maintenance calendars to avoid false calm when caches flush.

Mixed-source dashboards that blend Loki and Prometheus increase tail latency when one backend stalls; use `datasource` lanes in this search to see which family dominates `panel_query_p99_ms`.

Image renderer saturation often correlates with alert storms, not with interactive browsing; when severity rises on `/render` lanes, route to renderer runbooks distinct from datasource tuning.

Grafana database performance shows up in `grafana_database_query_milliseconds_count` spikes; when API histograms lag but DB counters explode, prioritize SQL explain plans and index hygiene before rewriting panels.

Provisioned dashboards that hot-reload from Git may spike load across many UIDs; use `provisioning_source` metadata from CMDB joins to explain fleet-wide elevation.

Kubernetes CPU throttling on Grafana pods elevates both access and metrics arms; correlate with cAdvisor or kube metrics before declaring query toxicity.

Nginx upstream retries can inflate `duration_ms` without changing Grafana CPU; inspect upstream error logs alongside Grafana logs.

Enterprise anonymization features for snapshots change auth_kind distributions; revisit thresholds when anonymous org policies shift.

FinOps stakeholders appreciate exports that tie `sla_class` to load regressions so cost conversations include customer-visible latency.

Security teams should ensure runbook URLs in inventory do not leak internal-only wiki paths to broad Splunk roles.

Compliance narratives benefit from archived weekly CSV snapshots filtered to critical and high severities with change ticket references when Grafana versions move.

Training drills should rehearse interpreting multisearch rows when only the RUM lane moves versus only the access lane, so on-call engineers do not confuse frontend regressions with backend query stalls.

Long-term improvement: promote recording rules for any query that repeatedly appears as `max_panel_query_ms` in this search for multiple weeks.

Documentation: when renaming `dashboard_uid` values in inventory, backfill summaries or accept brief breaks in longitudinal charts rather than silently mapping old IDs to new teams.

Governance: require peer review for imports that enable Repeat by variable on high-cardinality template defaults.

Continuous improvement: after incidents, capture before-and-after snapshots of `load_time_p95_ms` and `panel_query_p99_ms` to prove return to steady state.

Stakeholder communication: product owners care about missed operational moments more than histograms; translate severity rows into probable customer impact using `sla_class` and `runbook_url` pointers.

Tooling: optional Splunk ITSI modules can map dashboards to services when KPIs consume the same metrics indexes, but this UC remains valuable without premium modules.

Post-incident prompts: ask whether a plugin upgrade coincided with RUM LCP movement, whether provisioning reloads coincided with fleet-wide spikes, and whether auth proxy latency changed during IdP maintenance before closing tickets.

Vendor support bundles should include redacted nginx snippets, Grafana metrics scrape configs, and Splunk search results for the alert window.

Edge cases include IPv6-only clients behind translators that inflate RTT; segment by network path when policy permits.

Rate limits at Grafana HTTP layers can slow dashboard GETs during automation storms; correlate API logs separately from human traffic.

Cold starts after node drains can spike histograms until caches warm; pair with Kubernetes maintenance timelines before paging application teams.

Windows-hosted Grafana remains rare in this corpus; Linux and Kubernetes patterns dominate operational guidance.

Mainframe bridges are out of scope unless Prometheus exporters front those systems; document unusual bridges in inventory notes.

Cloud vendor FaaS-hosted Grafana is uncommon; ephemeral UIDs require inventory patterns that tolerate churn.

Finally, human judgment still validates automated severity; this search guides triage rather than replacing context from incident commanders.

Appendix note: pair with UC-13.6.2 when panels error rather than merely slow; pair with UC-13.5.9 when your own web property wraps Grafana and already emits rich RUM spans under first-party routes.

Appendix note: document Grafana feature toggles that affect caching and query splitting so validators interpret regressions after toggle flips.

Appendix note: when Tempo queries contribute latency, ensure traceQL panels carry sane limits analogous to log panels.

Appendix note: duplicate scrapers of `/metrics` can inflate histogram samples; dedupe architecturally before raising thresholds.

Appendix note: legal reviewers may require minimization of raw query text in evidence packs; store hashes instead of full strings when policy demands.

Appendix note: teach executives that anonymous kiosk boards may legitimately diverge from authenticated SLOs; use `auth_kind` reasoning before declaring security incidents.

Appendix note: PNG renderer scaling guidelines belong beside this saved search in the same portal page as Grafana core scaling guidance.

Appendix note: OpenTelemetry browser instrumentation can augment optional RUM lanes without replacing access log truth for API latency.

Appendix note: summarize monthly counts of sustained_load_critical breaches by owner_team for capacity reviews.

Appendix note: integrate maintenance metadata from change systems to auto-suppress known cache flush windows only with written approval.

Appendix note: for hybrid estates, ensure on-prem and cloud Grafana instances use distinct grafana_instance labels to avoid accidental merges in stats.

Appendix note: revisit slowest_panel_ratio thresholds quarterly as median dashboard complexity grows fleet-wide.
spl:
`comment("UC-13.6.1 Grafana dashboard load P95 panel query P99 anon vs auth render slowest_panel_ratio grafana:access grafana:logs mstats RUM optional earliest=-4h@h bucket=5m stream_window=12")`
| multisearch
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform) sourcetype=grafana:access earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval uri=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,request_uri,url_path,path,""))))
      | where like(uri,"%/api/dashboards/uid/%") OR like(uri,"%/d/%") OR like(uri,"%/render%")
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,splunk_server,"unknown_grafana"))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval user_f=trim(toString(coalesce(user,remote_user,username,http_user,"")))
      | eval auth_kind=if(len(user_f)<2 OR user_f=="-" OR match(lower(user_f),"anonymous|anon|guest|public"),"anon","auth")
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,dashboardUid,uid,"")))
      | rex field=uri max_match=1 "/api/dashboards/uid/(?<rex_uid>[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)"
      | eval dashboard_uid=if(len(dashboard_uid)<2 AND len(rex_uid)>1,rex_uid,dashboard_uid)
      | rex field=uri max_match=1 "/d/(?<rex_dash>[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+)/"
      | eval dashboard_uid=if(len(dashboard_uid)<2 AND len(rex_dash)>1,rex_dash,dashboard_uid)
      | eval load_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,request_time_ms,latency_ms,upstream_response_time_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval load_ms=if(load_ms>0 AND load_ms<3000000,load_ms,null())
      | eval panel_id=trim(toString(coalesce(panel_id,"access_aggregate")))
      | eval datasource=trim(toString(coalesce(datasource,"nginx_access_path")))
      | eval query_ms=null()
      | eval err_hit=if(match(toString(coalesce(status_code,http_status,status,"200")),"^(4|5)"),1,0)
      | eval lane="access_auth"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability OR index=platform) sourcetype=grafana:logs earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval lr=lower(toString(coalesce(_raw,"")))
      | eval lg=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(logger,component,""))))
      | where like(lg,"%tsdb.prometheus%") OR like(lg,"%tsdb.loki%") OR like(lg,"%plugin.querydata%") OR match(lr,"querydata|tsdb\\.prometheus|tsdb\\.loki")
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,splunk_server,"unknown_grafana"))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,dashboardUid,uid,"panel_unknown")))
      | eval panel_id=trim(toString(coalesce(panelId,panel_id,"0")))
      | eval datasource=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(datasource,datasource_type,ds_type,"mixed"))))
      | eval query_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,query_duration_ms,elapsed_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval query_ms=if(query_ms>0 AND query_ms<600000,query_ms,null())
      | eval load_ms=null()
      | eval auth_kind="backend"
      | eval err_hit=if(match(lr,"error|eror|fail|timeout|deadline|canceled"),1,0)
      | eval lane="tsdb_plugin_logs"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ | mstats avg(_value) AS hist_ms WHERE index=metrics OR index=prometheus_metrics OR index=observability_metrics (metric_name="grafana_api_dashboard_get_milliseconds_bucket" OR metric_name="grafana_api_dashboard_save_milliseconds_bucket") span=5m BY host metric_name le
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,instance,"metrics_host"))))
      | eval org_id="0"
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,"metrics_aggregate")))
      | eval panel_id="metrics_histogram"
      | eval datasource="grafana_internal_metrics"
      | eval le_num=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(le,le_bucket,"0"))),10)
      | eval query_ms=null()
      | eval load_ms=if(isnotnull(le_num),le_num,null())
      | eval auth_kind="metrics"
      | eval err_hit=0
      | eval lane="grafana_api_histogram"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=splunk_rum OR index=webperf OR index=observability) (sourcetype=splunk_rum:span OR sourcetype=browser:synthetic) earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval svc=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(service.name,service,""))))
      | eval app_l=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(app,application,""))))
      | where match(svc,"grafana") OR match(app_l,"grafana") OR match(lower(coalesce(url_full,page_url,"")),"grafana")
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,deployment_environment,"rum_grafana"))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,"0")))
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,route,http_target,"rum_dashboard")))
      | eval panel_id="rum_navigation"
      | eval datasource="browser_rum"
      | eval load_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,page_load_ms,largest_contentful_paint_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval query_ms=null()
      | eval auth_kind=if(match(lower(coalesce(user_status,auth_state,"")),"anon|guest|anonymous"),"anon","auth")
      | eval err_hit=if(match(lower(coalesce(error_type,exception_type,"")),"error|exception"),1,0)
      | eval lane="grafana_rum_optional"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
    [ search (index=grafana OR index=observability) sourcetype=grafana:access earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval uri=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(uri,request_uri,url_path,path,""))))
      | where like(uri,"%/public/dashboards%") OR like(uri,"%/api/public%")
      | eval grafana_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(instance,host,splunk_server,"unknown_grafana"))))
      | eval org_id=trim(toString(coalesce(orgId,org_id,org,"0")))
      | eval user_f="anonymous_viewer"
      | eval auth_kind="anon"
      | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,"public_board")))
      | eval load_ms=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce(duration_ms,request_time_ms,latency_ms,"0"))),10)
      | eval panel_id="public_surface"
      | eval datasource="anonymous_edge"
      | eval query_ms=null()
      | eval err_hit=if(match(toString(coalesce(status_code,http_status,status,"200")),"^(4|5)"),1,0)
      | eval lane="access_anon_bias"
      | bucket _time span=5m ]
| eval grafana_instance=coalesce(grafana_instance,"unknown_grafana")
| eval dashboard_uid=coalesce(nullif(dashboard_uid,""),"unknown_uid")
| eval panel_id=coalesce(panel_id,"0")
| eval datasource=coalesce(datasource,"mixed")
| eval org_id=coalesce(org_id,"0")
| eval auth_kind=coalesce(auth_kind,"auth")
| stats
    perc95(load_ms) AS load_time_p95_ms
    perc99(query_ms) AS panel_query_p99_ms
    max(query_ms) AS max_panel_query_ms
    count AS evt_count
    dc(panel_id) AS panel_count
    sum(err_hit) AS err_hits
    perc95(eval(if(auth_kind=="anon",load_ms,null()))) AS anon_load_p95_ms
    perc95(eval(if(auth_kind=="auth",load_ms,null()))) AS auth_load_p95_ms
  BY _time grafana_instance org_id dashboard_uid datasource lane
| eval error_panel_pct=if(evt_count>0,round(100*err_hits/evt_count,2),0)
| eval slowest_panel_ratio=round(max_panel_query_ms/nullif(load_time_p95_ms,0),4)
| eval slowest_panel_ratio=if(isnull(slowest_panel_ratio),0,slowest_panel_ratio)
| sort 0 grafana_instance dashboard_uid datasource - _time
| streamstats window=12 current=t global=f
    first(load_time_p95_ms) AS load_p95_lead
    first(max_panel_query_ms) AS max_q_lead
    sum(eval(if(load_time_p95_ms>5000,1,0))) AS over5k_buckets
  BY grafana_instance dashboard_uid
| eval sustained_load_critical=if(over5k_buckets>=6,1,0)
| eventstats median(load_time_p95_ms) AS env_load_med BY grafana_instance
| eval severity=case(
    (sustained_load_critical==1 AND load_time_p95_ms>5000) OR (slowest_panel_ratio>0.6 AND panel_query_p99_ms>8000),"critical",
    load_time_p95_ms>3500 OR panel_query_p99_ms>5000 OR slowest_panel_ratio>0.45 OR error_panel_pct>8 OR (anon_load_p95_ms>0 AND auth_load_p95_ms>0 AND anon_load_p95_ms<(auth_load_p95_ms*0.55)),"high",
    load_time_p95_ms>2200 OR panel_query_p99_ms>2500 OR error_panel_pct>3 OR load_time_p95_ms>(env_load_med*1.6),"medium",
    true(),"low")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    severity=="critical" AND slowest_panel_ratio>0.6,"rewrite_or_shard_heaviest_panel_raise_recording_rules_narrow_time_range",
    severity=="critical" AND sustained_load_critical==1,"investigate_grafana_db_and_api_path_scale_replicas_review_provisioning_reload_storms",
    match(datasource,"loki"),"tighten_logql_line_filters_reduce_scan_width_review_chunk_and_query_limits",
    match(datasource,"prometheus|mimir"),"inspect_federation_and_remote_read_paths_dedupe_high_cardinality_queries",
    match(datasource,"browser_rum"),"profile_frontend_bundle_and_plugin_assets_validate_cdn_cache_headers",
    match(lane,"access_anon_bias"),"compare_anon_versus_auth_paths_tune_cookie_and_oauth_proxy_overhead",
    true(),"open_grafana_query_inspector_correlate_nginx_grafana_logs_and_metrics")
| join type=left max=0 dashboard_uid
    [| inputlookup grafana_dashboard_inventory.csv
     | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,uid,"")))
     | fields dashboard_uid dashboard_title owner_team business_priority sla_class runbook_url last_reviewed]
| join type=left max=0 dashboard_uid
    [| search index=cmdb sourcetype=grafana_dashboard_metadata earliest=-24h latest=now
     | eval dashboard_uid=trim(toString(coalesce(dashboard_uid,uid,"")))
     | dedup dashboard_uid
     | fields dashboard_uid environment provisioning_source]
| eval dashboard_title=coalesce(dashboard_title,dashboard_uid)
| eval owner_team=coalesce(owner_team,"unassigned")
| eval sla_class=coalesce(sla_class,business_priority,"standard")
| table _time grafana_instance dashboard_uid dashboard_title owner_team sla_class load_time_p95_ms panel_query_p99_ms slowest_panel_ratio error_panel_pct severity recommended_action
cimSpl:
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Web.status) AS web_status latest(Web.url) AS web_url FROM datamodel=Web WHERE nodename=Web earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Web.src
| rename Web.src AS client_ip
| join type=left max=0 client_ip [| tstats summariesonly=true perc95(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_p95 FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host | rename Performance.host AS client_ip]
| table client_ip web_status web_url cpu_p95
dataModelAcceleration: Accelerate Web summaries for Grafana ingress URLs when field mappings align; accelerate Performance summaries for hosts mapped to Grafana pods and render workers when correlating tail latency with CPU saturation; retain thirty days for quarterly observability attestations.
visualization: Severity-colored table by grafana_instance and dashboard_uid with drilldowns to grafana:access, grafana:logs, mstats histogram rows, and optional RUM spans; dual-axis trends for load_time_p95_ms and panel_query_p99_ms; heatmap of slowest_panel_ratio by datasource.
knownFalsePositives: Scheduled load tests that replay heavy dashboards will elevate P95 and P99 percentiles without customer impact; tag lab org identifiers in inventory and suppress production paging for those keys. Marketing-period dashboard sharing spikes anonymous cohort traffic and can widen anon versus auth deltas that look like proxy bugs until calendars confirm a campaign. Infrastructure cold-cache after maintenance widens histograms for thirty to ninety minutes; pair bursts with change tickets before rewriting queries. Dashboard imports during executive demos intentionally load maximal boards; gate demos with non-production instances or temporary alert overrides. Mobile users on poor cellular links inflate optional RUM spans without implying Grafana backend regressions; segment by network quality when telemetry exists. Federated Prometheus failovers can shift latency between regions without code changes; compare federation health before muting. Grafana plugin rollback windows sometimes show mixed asset versions on edge caches; treat short spikes as expected when vendor guidance predicts CDN eventual consistency. Penetration-test traffic against public snapshots resembles anonymous storms; compare against approved security calendars. Duplicate scrapers of Grafana metrics can distort internal histogram arms until dedupe fixes land architecturally. Very low traffic dashboards produce unstable percentiles with tiny denominators; require minimum evt_count floors in alert wrappers beyond this exploratory search.
hardware: Applies to Grafana containers on Kubernetes, Linux virtual machines running Grafana OSS or Enterprise, managed Grafana Cloud stacks, nginx or Envoy ingress tiers terminating TLS in front of Grafana, and optional browser clients loading Grafana in iframes across laptop and NOC wall displays.
exclusions: Does not replace UC-13.6.2 data source query failure analytics, UC-13.6.3 Unified Alerting evaluation duration analytics, UC-13.6.5 Grafana authentication anomaly analytics, UC-13.6.13 notifier delivery failure analytics, deep database index tuning beyond correlation hints, or first-party Splunk RUM product analytics for non-Grafana web applications absent optional Grafana-tagged spans.

controlTest.positiveScenario: In a lab Grafana stack, import a community dashboard with an unbounded logs table, drive concurrent refreshes from nginx access logs with duration_ms above five seconds for thirty minutes while tsdb logs show panel_query_p99_ms above eight seconds, and confirm observability_uc_13_6_1_grafana_dashboard_load_panel_perf returns critical or high severity within two five-minute windows with non-null recommended_action.
controlTest.negativeScenario: After removing the toxic panel, restoring healthy cache headers, and returning access P95 under two seconds with quiet error_panel_pct, confirm severities remain low for steady state and the alert wrapper emits zero rows when filtering to critical and high only for thirty consecutive runs.

evidence: Saved search observability_uc_13_6_1_grafana_dashboard_load_panel_perf on a five-minute schedule; observability dashboard drilldowns across grafana:access, grafana:logs, mstats Grafana API histogram metrics, and optional RUM spans; alert routing to platform observability on-call; weekly CSV exports archived to a restricted evidence index.
